articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Environmental Clearance for Key Infrastructure Project with Stringent Conditions
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, upheld the environmental clearance granted to a major national infrastructure project, emphasizing the balance between development and environmental protection.
• The court mandated the implementation of additional, stringent compensatory afforestation and biodiversity conservation measures.
• It highlighted the principle of sustainable development as a guiding factor for project approvals.
• The ruling also reiterated the importance of public participation and expert committee assessments in environmental impact evaluations.
• This decision is expected to set a precedent for future large-scale developmental projects requiring environmental sanctions.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Judiciary
Environmental protection acts
Sustainable development
prelimsPoints: The Supreme Court's role in environmental jurisprudence.
Key provisions of the Environment Protection Act, 1986.
Concept of 'Public Trust Doctrine' in environmental law.
Types of environmental clearances required for large projects.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in balancing economic development and environmental conservation in India.
Analyze the role of the judiciary in strengthening environmental governance and ensuring sustainable development.
Evaluate the effectiveness of Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) processes in India and suggest reforms.
Examine the implications of such judicial pronouncements on future infrastructure projects and investment.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of Supreme Court building with a backdrop of a green forest and infrastructure project, symbolizing balance.
articles.trivia: India was one of the first countries to make environmental protection a constitutional mandate through the 42nd Amendment Act, 1976.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu Train Collision: 19 Injured, Rail Safety Concerns Resurface
articles.explainer:
• A Mysuru-Darbhanga train collided with a freight train on a loop line near Chennai, resulting in injuries to 19 passengers.
• The incident occurred due to a suspected signalling error or human oversight, prompting a high-level inquiry by railway authorities.
• This collision has reignited debates over the need for advanced safety mechanisms like Kavach (Train Collision Avoidance System) across the entire rail network.
• Railway Minister assured swift action and compensation for the injured, emphasizing zero tolerance for safety lapses.
• The accident highlights the persistent challenges in maintaining operational safety on India's vast and busy railway network.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Railways
Disaster and disaster management
Internal security challenges
prelimsPoints: Location of the collision: Chennai, Tamil Nadu.
Number of injured: 19.
Name of the train involved: Mysuru-Darbhanga Express.
Kavach system: indigenous automatic train protection system developed by RDSO.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major challenges faced by Indian Railways in ensuring passenger safety and suggest measures to overcome them.
Analyze the role of technology, such as Kavach, in enhancing railway safety and its implementation challenges.
Examine the institutional mechanisms for investigating railway accidents and implementing recommendations.
Evaluate the human factor in railway accidents and the importance of training and compliance with safety protocols.
articles.imageDescription: Emergency services attending to a derailed train coach at a collision site.
articles.trivia: Indian Railways is the fourth-largest railway network in the world, transporting billions of passengers annually.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Economic and Social Development
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Energy, Ports, Roads, Railways, Airports, etc.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Strategic Partnership Summit Concludes: Focus on Trade, Maritime Security, and Digital Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with leaders reaffirming their commitment to strengthening the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.
• Key discussions revolved around boosting bilateral trade to achieve the USD 200 billion target, enhancing maritime security cooperation in the Indo-Pacific, and promoting digital transformation.
• India emphasized its 'Act East' policy, reiterating support for ASEAN centrality in the regional architecture.
• Agreements were signed on cooperation in renewable energy, smart cities, and public health.
• The summit underscored the importance of a free, open, and rules-based Indo-Pacific region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: ASEAN founding members and current members.
Pillars of India-ASEAN cooperation.
Significance of the Indo-Pacific region.
Key initiatives under India's Act East Policy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of India’s 'Act East' policy and its significance for regional stability and economic growth.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in India-ASEAN economic cooperation, particularly in the post-pandemic era.
Examine India's role in promoting maritime security and a rules-based order in the Indo-Pacific region.
Evaluate the strategic importance of ASEAN for India's foreign policy and security interests.
articles.imageDescription: Leaders of India and ASEAN member states shaking hands at a summit.
articles.trivia: ASEAN was established on 8 August 1967 in Bangkok, Thailand, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration).

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Inflation Concerns, Signals Cautious Stance
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach to manage persistent inflationary pressures, particularly in food prices, while supporting economic growth.
• RBI Governor highlighted global uncertainties and domestic demand-supply dynamics as key factors influencing the policy stance.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the 4% target within a flexible inflation targeting framework.
• Experts anticipate continued vigilance from RBI, with future rate cuts dependent on a sustained decline in inflation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: Current Repo Rate: 6.5%.
Composition of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC).
Primary objective of RBI's monetary policy.
Definition of Repo Rate and its impact on lending rates.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in managing inflation while supporting economic growth in India.
Analyze the effectiveness of the flexible inflation targeting framework in achieving price stability in India.
Examine the various tools of monetary policy used by the RBI and their impact on the Indian economy.
Evaluate the role of global economic factors and domestic supply-side issues in influencing India's inflation trajectory.
articles.imageDescription: RBI building with financial graphs overlaid, depicting economic indicators.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and consists of six members: three officials of the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful autonomous landing experiment of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) 'Pushpak' at the Aeronautical Test Range in Chitradurga.
• This test marks a significant step towards developing a cost-effective and sustainable space transportation system for India.
• The RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) program aims to master critical technologies like hypersonic flight, autonomous landing, and winged body re-entry.
• Successful development of RLV technology will enable ISRO to reduce launch costs significantly and enhance India's competitive edge in the global space market.
• Future missions will involve orbital re-entry and return flight experiments.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Name of ISRO's RLV-TD: Pushpak.
Location of the test: Aeronautical Test Range, Chitradurga.
Key technologies demonstrated: autonomous landing, re-entry.
Objective of RLV program: cost reduction, sustainability.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's space program and its potential economic benefits.
Analyze how ISRO's indigenous development of space technology contributes to India's strategic autonomy and global standing.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in the commercial space sector, particularly with technologies like RLV.
Evaluate the role of space technology in national development and security.
articles.imageDescription: ISRO's 'Pushpak' Reusable Launch Vehicle gliding for an autonomous landing.
articles.trivia: ISRO's first RLV-TD test was conducted in 2016, marking a significant milestone in India's space journey.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Science and Technology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Space missions, satellites and their applications

articles.headline: Government Launches 'Gram Samriddhi' Scheme to Boost Rural Livelihoods and Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government launched the 'Gram Samriddhi' scheme, a comprehensive initiative aimed at enhancing rural livelihoods and developing critical infrastructure in villages.
• The scheme focuses on improving access to credit for self-help groups (SHGs), promoting skill development, and supporting micro-enterprises.
• It integrates various existing rural development programs to ensure synergistic implementation and maximize impact.
• Special provisions are included for vulnerable sections and tribal areas to ensure inclusive growth.
• The scheme targets creation of sustainable employment opportunities and reduction of rural-urban migration.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
keyTerms: Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM)
prelimsPoints: Name of the scheme: 'Gram Samriddhi'.
Primary objective: enhancing rural livelihoods and infrastructure.
Target beneficiaries: rural population, SHGs, micro-enterprises.
Key focus areas: credit access, skill development, integrated programs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of rural development schemes in achieving inclusive growth and poverty alleviation in India.
Analyze the potential of the 'Gram Samriddhi' scheme to address challenges like unemployment and migration in rural areas.
Evaluate the role of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) in empowering rural women and fostering economic development.
Suggest measures to improve the implementation and effectiveness of integrated rural development programs in India.
articles.imageDescription: A vibrant village scene depicting people engaged in various livelihood activities and modern infrastructure.
articles.trivia: Mahatma Gandhi's vision of 'Gram Swaraj' emphasized self-sufficient and self-governing villages as the foundation of India's development.

articles.headline: UN Report Highlights India's Progress in SDG 4 (Quality Education) but Flags Challenges
articles.explainer:
• A recent UN report acknowledged India's significant strides towards achieving Sustainable Development Goal 4 (Quality Education), particularly in increasing access to primary education.
• The report praised initiatives like the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 and Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan for improving enrollment rates and infrastructure.
• However, it also pointed out persistent challenges, including disparities in learning outcomes, digital divide, and quality of higher education.
• The report recommended increased investment in teacher training, early childhood care, and technology integration to bridge existing gaps.
• India's efforts are crucial for global progress towards SDG 4, given its large youth population.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: SDG 4: Quality Education.
Key Indian policies mentioned: NEP 2020, Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan.
Challenges highlighted: learning outcomes, digital divide, higher education quality.
UN as the source of the report.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's progress and challenges in achieving Sustainable Development Goal 4 (Quality Education).
Discuss how the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 aims to address the identified gaps in India's education system.
Examine the impact of the digital divide on educational equity in India, especially in the context of online learning.
Suggest policy interventions to improve learning outcomes and ensure inclusive and equitable quality education for all.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a classroom, with the UN logo subtly in the background, symbolizing global goals.
articles.trivia: The 17 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) were adopted by all UN member states in 2015 as part of the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education

articles.headline: New Draft Data Protection Rules Released for Public Consultation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has released the draft rules under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023, for public feedback.
• These rules elaborate on the procedures for data fiduciaries to process personal data, mechanism for grievance redressal, and obligations of data processors.
• Key provisions include detailed guidelines on data breach notification, consent requirements, and the establishment of the Data Protection Board of India.
• The move aims to operationalize the DPDP Act, ensuring robust data privacy and security for Indian citizens.
• Stakeholders have been invited to submit their comments and suggestions within a 30-day period.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Cyber security
Role of IT in governance
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: Act under which rules are framed: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
Ministry responsible: MeitY.
Key entity to be established: Data Protection Board of India.
Concept of 'data fiduciary' and 'data principal'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, in safeguarding citizen privacy in the digital age.
Analyze the key provisions of the newly released draft rules and their potential impact on businesses and individuals.
Examine the challenges in implementing a robust data protection framework in a digitally transforming economy like India's.
Evaluate the balance struck between individual privacy rights and legitimate state/business interests under the new law.
articles.imageDescription: Digital lock icon with binary code, symbolizing data security and privacy.
articles.trivia: The right to privacy was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court of India in the Puttaswamy judgment of 2017.

articles.headline: Himalayan Region Faces Increased Flood Risk Due to Glacial Lake Outbursts, Warns IMD
articles.explainer:
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued a warning regarding an increased risk of Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs) in the Himalayan region.
• Rising global temperatures are accelerating glacier melt, leading to the formation and expansion of unstable glacial lakes.
• GLOFs pose a severe threat to downstream communities and infrastructure, as evidenced by recent events in the region.
• IMD emphasized the need for enhanced early warning systems, real-time monitoring of glacial lakes, and disaster preparedness.
• Collaborative efforts with neighboring countries and international organizations are crucial for effective risk mitigation.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Definition of Glacial Lake Outburst Flood (GLOF).
Primary cause of GLOFs: glacier melt due to global warming.
Agency issuing the warning: IMD.
Region most affected: Himalayan region.
mainsPoints: Discuss the causes and consequences of Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs) in the Himalayan region.
Analyze the impact of climate change on mountain ecosystems and the increasing frequency of extreme weather events.
Examine the current disaster management strategies for GLOFs and suggest improvements in early warning and mitigation.
Evaluate the need for regional cooperation among Himalayan countries to address transboundary climate-induced hazards.
articles.imageDescription: A satellite image showing a glacial lake in the Himalayas with potential flood paths.
articles.trivia: The Himalayas contain the largest concentration of glaciers outside the polar regions, often referred to as the 'Third Pole'.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Geography
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc.

articles.headline: Defence Ministry Approves Indigenous Procurement of Naval Utility Helicopters
articles.explainer:
• The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC), chaired by the Defence Minister, approved the procurement of a batch of indigenously designed and manufactured Naval Utility Helicopters (NUH).
• This decision is a major boost to the 'Make in India' and 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives in the defence sector.
• The NUHs will replace the aging fleet of Chetak helicopters, enhancing the operational capabilities of the Indian Navy for reconnaissance, search & rescue, and light transport missions.
• The procurement will involve significant participation from Indian private sector defence manufacturers, fostering domestic industry growth.
• This move aligns with the long-term vision of achieving self-reliance in critical defence technologies and reducing import dependency.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Various security forces and agencies and their mandate
Indigenization of technology
prelimsPoints: Body approving procurement: Defence Acquisition Council (DAC).
Type of helicopter: Naval Utility Helicopter (NUH).
Policy initiatives promoted: Make in India, Aatmanirbhar Bharat.
Helicopters being replaced: Chetak helicopters.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenization in India's defence sector for national security and economic growth.
Analyze the progress and challenges of the 'Make in India' and 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives in achieving self-reliance in defence production.
Examine the role of private sector participation in boosting India's defence manufacturing capabilities.
Evaluate the strategic implications of modernizing the Indian Navy's fleet with advanced indigenous platforms.
articles.imageDescription: A modern naval utility helicopter flying over a warship at sea.
articles.trivia: India is one of the world's largest importers of defence equipment, but efforts are underway to significantly increase domestic production.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Various security forces and agencies and their mandate

articles.headline: Centre-State Financial Relations: 16th Finance Commission Holds Consultations with States
articles.explainer:
• The 16th Finance Commission, currently underway, held its latest round of consultations with various state governments to understand their fiscal positions and recommendations.
• Discussions focused on the devolution of taxes, grants-in-aid, and measures to improve the fiscal health of states and local bodies.
• States presented their revenue projections, expenditure needs, and specific requests for sector-specific grants and disaster relief funds.
• The Commission's recommendations are crucial for shaping India's federal fiscal architecture for the next five years.
• Key areas of deliberation include horizontal and vertical devolution formulae, performance-based incentives, and debt management.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Finance Commission – its functions.
prelimsPoints: Constitutional article for Finance Commission: Article 280.
Current Finance Commission: 16th.
Key functions: recommending devolution of taxes, grants-in-aid.
Period of recommendations: five years.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and significance of the Finance Commission in shaping fiscal federalism in India.
Analyze the key challenges faced by states in managing their finances and their expectations from the 16th Finance Commission.
Examine the evolving dynamics of Centre-State financial relations, especially in the context of GST and increased devolution.
Suggest reforms to enhance the effectiveness of the Finance Commission's recommendations and improve fiscal discipline across all tiers of government.
articles.imageDescription: Indian currency notes arranged to represent Centre-State financial flow, with the Finance Commission logo.
articles.trivia: The first Finance Commission was constituted in 1951 under the chairmanship of K.C. Neogy.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) Unearths Ancient Artifacts in Karnataka
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a significant cache of ancient artifacts during an excavation in the Raichur district of Karnataka.
• The findings include pottery shards, ancient coins, iron tools, and remnants of what appears to be an early medieval settlement.
• Preliminary analysis suggests that the site could provide crucial insights into the cultural and economic life of the region between the 6th and 10th centuries CE.
• The discovery is expected to shed light on the lesser-known aspects of South Indian history and trade routes.
• Further excavations and carbon dating are underway to establish the precise chronology and significance of the site.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
History of India
prelimsPoints: Agency responsible for discovery: ASI.
Location of discovery: Raichur district, Karnataka.
Types of artifacts found: pottery, coins, iron tools.
Period indicated: 6th-10th centuries CE (Early Medieval).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing the social, economic, and cultural history of ancient and early medieval India.
Analyze the role of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in preserving and protecting India's cultural heritage.
Examine how new findings contribute to revising existing historical narratives and understanding regional specificities.
Evaluate the challenges faced in archaeological excavations and preservation of sites in India.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating artifacts at a historical site in Karnataka.
articles.trivia: The ASI was founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham, who became its first Director-General.

articles.headline: Election Commission of India Proposes Measures to Enhance Transparency in Political Funding
articles.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) has submitted a set of proposals to the government aimed at bringing greater transparency to political funding.
• The recommendations include capping anonymous donations, mandating disclosure of donors for all contributions, and strengthening the audit of political parties' accounts.
• ECI highlighted concerns regarding the opacity of electoral bonds and the need for comprehensive reforms to ensure a level playing field.
• The proposals are part of ECI's ongoing efforts to streamline electoral processes and reduce the influence of 'black money' in elections.
• Implementation of these reforms would require amendments to existing laws, including the Representation of the People Act, 1951.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Body proposing reforms: ECI.
Key area of reforms: political funding.
Specific proposals: capping anonymous donations, donor disclosure.
Act needing amendment: Representation of the People Act, 1951.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges posed by opaque political funding to democratic governance in India.
Analyze the Election Commission of India's role in promoting free and fair elections and its efforts towards electoral reforms.
Evaluate the effectiveness of electoral bonds in enhancing transparency in political funding and suggest alternatives.
Examine the constitutional and legal framework governing political funding in India and propose comprehensive reforms.
articles.imageDescription: A ballot box with currency notes, symbolizing the intersection of elections and money, with ECI logo.
articles.trivia: Article 324 of the Indian Constitution provides for an Election Commission to superintend, direct and control elections.

articles.headline: India's Energy Transition: Focus on Green Hydrogen Mission Implementation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy emphasized accelerated implementation of the National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM) to achieve India's climate goals.
• NGHM aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production, utilization, and export, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• Key focus areas include developing green hydrogen production capacity, promoting its use in hard-to-abate sectors like steel and cement, and creating a robust ecosystem for research and development.
• The mission envisages significant investments, job creation, and a reduction in greenhouse gas emissions.
• Public-private partnerships and international collaborations are being actively pursued to scale up green hydrogen initiatives.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Infrastructure: Energy
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: Mission name: National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM).
Ministry responsible: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy.
Objective: India as a global hub for green hydrogen.
Process for green hydrogen: electrolysis using renewable energy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and achieving its climate change commitments.
Analyze the key pillars and targets of the National Green Hydrogen Mission and its potential impact on various sectors of the economy.
Examine the technological, economic, and infrastructure challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the role of government policies, incentives, and international collaborations in accelerating India's green hydrogen ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: Stylized hydrogen atom with green leaves, symbolizing green hydrogen as a clean energy source.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

articles.headline: Nationwide Mental Health Awareness Campaign Launched Across States
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, in collaboration with state governments, launched a comprehensive nationwide mental health awareness campaign.
• The campaign aims to destigmatize mental illness, promote early intervention, and improve access to mental healthcare services.
• It involves public workshops, digital campaigns, and training for frontline health workers to identify and support individuals with mental health concerns.
• Emphasis is placed on adolescent mental health and addressing the psychological impact of modern lifestyles.
• The initiative aligns with the National Mental Health Program and aims to bridge the treatment gap in India.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: Ministry involved: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare.
Objective: destigmatize mental illness, promote early intervention.
Key focus groups: adolescents, frontline health workers.
Related program: National Mental Health Program.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing burden of mental health issues in India and the challenges in providing adequate mental healthcare services.
Analyze the importance of public awareness campaigns in destigmatizing mental illness and promoting help-seeking behavior.
Examine the provisions of the Mental Healthcare Act, 2017, and its impact on protecting the rights of individuals with mental illness.
Suggest comprehensive strategies to strengthen India's mental health infrastructure and integrate mental healthcare into primary health services.
articles.imageDescription: Diverse group of people in a circle, symbolizing community support for mental well-being, with a green ribbon.
articles.trivia: The World Health Organization (WHO) defines mental health as 'a state of well-being in which an individual realizes his or her own abilities, can cope with the normal stresses of life, can work productively and fruitfully, and is able to make a contribution to his or her community.'

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health

articles.headline: India's Industrial Production Growth Moderates to 5.8% in August, Manufacturing Sector Shows Resilience
articles.explainer:
• India's Index of Industrial Production (IIP) grew by 5.8% in August 2024, a moderation from the previous month but indicating steady recovery.
• The manufacturing sector, a key component of IIP, recorded a growth of 6.1%, showing resilience despite global headwinds.
• Mining output expanded by 8.5%, while electricity generation saw a growth of 4.3%.
• Capital goods and infrastructure/construction goods segments registered healthy growth, signaling investment uptick.
• Analysts suggest that festival demand and government infrastructure spending will likely support industrial growth in the coming months, though inflation remains a concern.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Industrial policy and its effects on industrial growth
prelimsPoints: IIP growth rate for August 2024: 5.8%.
Sector with highest growth: Mining (8.5%).
Key component of IIP: Manufacturing.
Agency releasing IIP data: National Statistical Office (NSO).
mainsPoints: Analyze the recent trends in India's industrial production and identify the factors contributing to its growth or moderation.
Discuss the significance of the manufacturing sector for India's economic growth and employment generation.
Examine the government's policies and initiatives aimed at boosting industrial output and competitiveness.
Evaluate the impact of global economic uncertainties and domestic demand on India's industrial recovery trajectory.
articles.imageDescription: Factory smokestacks with graphs trending upwards, symbolizing industrial growth.
articles.trivia: The base year for the Index of Industrial Production (IIP) is currently 2011-12.

articles.headline: India and Brazil Strengthen South-South Cooperation with New Agreements
articles.explainer:
• India and Brazil held high-level bilateral talks, reaffirming their commitment to strengthening South-South Cooperation and multilateralism.
• New agreements were signed focusing on cooperation in agriculture technology, renewable energy, and space exploration.
• Both nations emphasized the importance of reforming global institutions like the UN Security Council and the WTO to reflect current global realities.
• Discussions also covered enhancing trade ties, investment flows, and collaboration on climate change initiatives.
• This partnership is crucial for amplifying the voice of the Global South on international platforms.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: Countries involved: India and Brazil.
Key concept: South-South Cooperation.
Areas of cooperation: agriculture, renewable energy, space.
Global institutions mentioned: UN Security Council, WTO.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of South-South Cooperation in shaping the new world order and India's role in it.
Analyze the potential for India-Brazil partnership to drive economic growth and technological innovation in the Global South.
Examine the common positions of India and Brazil on reforming global governance institutions and their implications for multilateralism.
Evaluate the strategic importance of BRICS and other groupings in enhancing the influence of emerging economies.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Brazil intertwined, symbolizing strong bilateral relations.
articles.trivia: India, Brazil, and South Africa (IBSA) form a unique trilateral cooperation forum to promote South-South Cooperation.

articles.headline: Prime Minister Inaugurates Major Irrigation Project in Rajasthan, Boosting Agricultural Productivity
articles.explainer:
• The Prime Minister inaugurated a significant irrigation project in Rajasthan, aimed at providing perennial water supply to drought-prone regions.
• The project involves a network of canals and reservoirs, utilizing river water to boost agricultural productivity and ensure water security for farmers.
• This initiative is expected to transform farming practices, enable multiple cropping cycles, and improve the socio-economic conditions of local communities.
• The government emphasized its commitment to schemes like 'Per Drop More Crop' and enhancing farmer incomes through sustainable water management.
• The project also incorporates modern water conservation techniques and community participation for efficient resource utilization.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of world’s physical geography

Distribution of key natural resources across the world (including South Asia and the Indian subcontinent); factors responsible for the location of primary, secondary, and tertiary sector industries in various parts of the world (including India).
Issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: Location of the project: Rajasthan.
Objective: perennial water supply, agricultural productivity.
Related scheme: 'Per Drop More Crop'.
Impact: multiple cropping cycles, improved farmer income.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of irrigation infrastructure in enhancing agricultural sustainability and food security in India, particularly in arid and semi-arid regions.
Analyze the challenges faced in water resource management in Rajasthan and how such projects aim to address them.
Examine the role of government schemes and policies in promoting efficient water use and improving farmer incomes.
Evaluate the environmental and socio-economic impacts of large-scale irrigation projects and the need for sustainable practices.
articles.imageDescription: A vast agricultural field being irrigated by a canal system in a dry landscape.
articles.trivia: Rajasthan is India's largest state by area and is predominantly arid and semi-arid, making irrigation projects vital for its agriculture.

articles.headline: New Species of Flowering Plant Discovered in Western Ghats, Highlights Biodiversity Hotspot Status
articles.explainer:
• Botanists from the Botanical Survey of India (BSI) announced the discovery of a new species of flowering plant in the biodiversity-rich Western Ghats.
• The newly identified plant, belonging to the genus Impatiens, was found in a remote forest patch in Kerala.
• This discovery underscores the immense biodiversity of the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
• Researchers emphasized the critical need for conservation efforts to protect these unique ecosystems from habitat loss and climate change.
• The finding contributes to the growing list of endemic species in the region, aiding in ecological research and conservation strategies.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: Location of discovery: Western Ghats, Kerala.
Type of discovery: new species of flowering plant (Impatiens).
Western Ghats status: UNESCO World Heritage Site, biodiversity hotspot.
Agency involved: Botanical Survey of India (BSI).
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of biodiversity hotspots like the Western Ghats and the threats they face.
Analyze the importance of discovering new species for scientific research and conservation planning.
Examine the government's efforts and challenges in protecting the unique flora and fauna of the Western Ghats.
Suggest sustainable development practices that can reconcile conservation with the livelihood needs of local communities in biodiversity-rich regions.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up photograph of a vibrant, newly discovered flowering plant in a lush forest setting.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayan mountains and are home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

highlights: Supreme Court upholds environmental clearance for a major infrastructure project, balancing development and conservation.
Tamil Nadu train collision injures 19, reigniting concerns over railway safety and the need for advanced systems like Kavach.
India-ASEAN Summit concludes, focusing on boosting trade, maritime security, and digital cooperation in the Indo-Pacific.
RBI maintains repo rate at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time, adopting a cautious stance amidst inflation pressures.
ISRO successfully tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) 'Pushpak', a major step towards cost-effective space access.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Gram Samriddhi' scheme, consider the following statements:
1. It is a scheme launched by the Ministry of Rural Development to enhance rural livelihoods and infrastructure.
2. It primarily focuses on providing urban employment opportunities to reduce rural-urban migration.
3. It integrates various existing rural development programs for synergistic implementation.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as 'Gram Samriddhi' is indeed launched by the Ministry of Rural Development to boost rural livelihoods and infrastructure. Statement 2 is incorrect because the scheme focuses on creating sustainable employment opportunities in rural areas and reducing rural-urban migration, not primarily providing urban employment. Statement 3 is correct as it integrates existing rural development programs for synergistic implementation.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for recommending the devolution of taxes and grants-in-aid between the Union and the States in India?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Finance Commission is a constitutional body established under Article 280 of the Indian Constitution, primarily responsible for making recommendations on the distribution of tax revenues between the Union and the States, and among the States themselves. NITI Aayog is a policy think tank, RBI is the central bank, and the GST Council is responsible for GST-related matters.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the 'Kavach' system:
1. It is an indigenous automatic train protection (ATP) system developed by ISRO.
2. It helps prevent train collisions by automatically applying brakes if the driver fails to respond.
3. It is currently implemented across the entire Indian railway network.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; Kavach is an indigenous ATP system developed by the Research Designs & Standards Organisation (RDSO) under Indian Railways, not ISRO. Statement 2 is correct; it is designed to prevent collisions by alerting the driver and automatically activating the braking system if needed. Statement 3 is incorrect; while its implementation is being fast-tracked, it is not yet across the entire Indian railway network.

prelimsMocks.question: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023, aims to establish which of the following bodies to ensure robust data privacy and security?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023, provides for the establishment of the Data Protection Board of India to enforce its provisions and adjudicate on matters related to data protection and privacy.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) are a collection of 17 interlinked global goals designed to be a 'blueprint to achieve a better and more sustainable future for all'. Zero Hunger (SDG 2), Quality Education (SDG 4), and Clean Water and Sanitation (SDG 6) are all part of the 17 SDGs. Space Exploration and Colonization is not an SDG.

mainsMocks.question: Evaluate the challenges in balancing economic development and environmental protection in India. Discuss how recent judicial interventions, like the Supreme Court's ruling on environmental clearances, contribute to achieving sustainable development goals.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment. GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors; Judiciary.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the dilemma of development vs. environment. Discuss environmental challenges (pollution, resource depletion, climate change impacts). Explain the need for development (poverty, infrastructure). Analyze the role of EIA and its shortcomings. Detail how the Supreme Court's ruling (balancing act, stringent conditions, sustainable development principle) impacts this balance. Conclude with suggestions for effective governance and public participation.

mainsMocks.question: The recent Tamil Nadu train collision has once again highlighted the persistent challenges in ensuring railway safety in India. Critically analyze the factors contributing to such accidents and suggest comprehensive technological and administrative reforms needed to enhance the safety of Indian Railways.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Railways; Disaster and disaster management. GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin with the context of the recent accident. Identify key factors: human error, signaling issues, aging infrastructure, inadequate technology adoption, maintenance gaps. Discuss current measures (e.g., Kavach, safety funds). Propose reforms: accelerated Kavach implementation, modernization of signaling, enhanced training, strict accountability, independent safety regulator, investment in infrastructure, public awareness. Conclude with the vision of a 'Zero Accident' railway network.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the strategic importance of Green Hydrogen for India's energy security and achieving its climate change commitments. What are the key challenges in scaling up Green Hydrogen production and adoption, and how does the National Green Hydrogen Mission aim to address them?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life; Infrastructure: Energy.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce Green Hydrogen as a clean fuel. Explain its strategic importance for energy security (reducing import dependency) and climate commitments (decarbonization, Net-Zero). Detail the National Green Hydrogen Mission's objectives (global hub, production capacity, utilization). Discuss challenges: high production cost, infrastructure, technology development, water availability, policy support. Conclude by evaluating the mission's potential to overcome these challenges and foster a sustainable energy future.

quickBytes.text: The 2024 Tamil Nadu train collision involved a Mysuru-Darbhanga train and a freight train.
The Supreme Court's ruling on environmental clearance emphasized the principle of sustainable development.
India's 'Act East' policy is a key driver for its engagement with ASEAN countries.
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) uses the Repo Rate as a key monetary policy tool to control inflation.
ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) 'Pushpak' is designed for cost-effective space transportation.
The 'Gram Samriddhi' scheme focuses on enhancing rural livelihoods and developing infrastructure.
SDG 4 of the United Nations aims to ensure inclusive and equitable quality education for all.
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, provides for the establishment of the Data Protection Board of India.
Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs) are a significant climate change-induced hazard in the Himalayas.
Article 280 of the Indian Constitution mandates the establishment of a Finance Commission.

GS1.headline: Himalayan Region Faces Increased Flood Risk Due to Glacial Lake Outbursts, Warns IMD
GS1.explainer:
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued a warning regarding an increased risk of Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs) in the Himalayan region.
• Rising global temperatures are accelerating glacier melt, leading to the formation and expansion of unstable glacial lakes.
• GLOFs pose a severe threat to downstream communities and infrastructure, as evidenced by recent events in the region.
• IMD emphasized the need for enhanced early warning systems, real-time monitoring of glacial lakes, and disaster preparedness.
• Collaborative efforts with neighboring countries and international organizations are crucial for effective risk mitigation.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Disaster and disaster management
prelimsPoints: Definition of Glacial Lake Outburst Flood (GLOF).
Primary cause of GLOFs: glacier melt due to global warming.
Agency issuing the warning: IMD.
Region most affected: Himalayan region.
mainsPoints: Discuss the causes and consequences of Glacial Lake Outburst Floods (GLOFs) in the Himalayan region.
Analyze the impact of climate change on mountain ecosystems and the increasing frequency of extreme weather events.
Examine the current disaster management strategies for GLOFs and suggest improvements in early warning and mitigation.
Evaluate the need for regional cooperation among Himalayan countries to address transboundary climate-induced hazards.
GS1.imageDescription: A satellite image showing a glacial lake in the Himalayas with potential flood paths.
GS1.trivia: The Himalayas contain the largest concentration of glaciers outside the polar regions, often referred to as the 'Third Pole'.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Geography
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) Unearths Ancient Artifacts in Karnataka
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a significant cache of ancient artifacts during an excavation in the Raichur district of Karnataka.
• The findings include pottery shards, ancient coins, iron tools, and remnants of what appears to be an early medieval settlement.
• Preliminary analysis suggests that the site could provide crucial insights into the cultural and economic life of the region between the 6th and 10th centuries CE.
• The discovery is expected to shed light on the lesser-known aspects of South Indian history and trade routes.
• Further excavations and carbon dating are underway to establish the precise chronology and significance of the site.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
History of India
prelimsPoints: Agency responsible for discovery: ASI.
Location of discovery: Raichur district, Karnataka.
Types of artifacts found: pottery, coins, iron tools.
Period indicated: 6th-10th centuries CE (Early Medieval).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing the social, economic, and cultural history of ancient and early medieval India.
Analyze the role of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in preserving and protecting India's cultural heritage.
Examine how new findings contribute to revising existing historical narratives and understanding regional specificities.
Evaluate the challenges faced in archaeological excavations and preservation of sites in India.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists carefully excavating artifacts at a historical site in Karnataka.
GS1.trivia: The ASI was founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham, who became its first Director-General.

GS1.headline: Prime Minister Inaugurates Major Irrigation Project in Rajasthan, Boosting Agricultural Productivity
GS1.explainer:
• The Prime Minister inaugurated a significant irrigation project in Rajasthan, aimed at providing perennial water supply to drought-prone regions.
• The project involves a network of canals and reservoirs, utilizing river water to boost agricultural productivity and ensure water security for farmers.
• This initiative is expected to transform farming practices, enable multiple cropping cycles, and improve the socio-economic conditions of local communities.
• The government emphasized its commitment to schemes like 'Per Drop More Crop' and enhancing farmer incomes through sustainable water management.
• The project also incorporates modern water conservation techniques and community participation for efficient resource utilization.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of world’s physical geography

Distribution of key natural resources across the world (including South Asia and the Indian subcontinent); factors responsible for the location of primary, secondary, and tertiary sector industries in various parts of the world (including India).
Issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: Location of the project: Rajasthan.
Objective: perennial water supply, agricultural productivity.
Related scheme: 'Per Drop More Crop'.
Impact: multiple cropping cycles, improved farmer income.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of irrigation infrastructure in enhancing agricultural sustainability and food security in India, particularly in arid and semi-arid regions.
Analyze the challenges faced in water resource management in Rajasthan and how such projects aim to address them.
Examine the role of government schemes and policies in promoting efficient water use and improving farmer incomes.
Evaluate the environmental and socio-economic impacts of large-scale irrigation projects and the need for sustainable practices.
GS1.imageDescription: A vast agricultural field being irrigated by a canal system in a dry landscape.
GS1.trivia: Rajasthan is India's largest state by area and is predominantly arid and semi-arid, making irrigation projects vital for its agriculture.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Environmental Clearance for Key Infrastructure Project with Stringent Conditions
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark judgment, upheld the environmental clearance granted to a major national infrastructure project, emphasizing the balance between development and environmental protection.
• The court mandated the implementation of additional, stringent compensatory afforestation and biodiversity conservation measures.
• It highlighted the principle of sustainable development as a guiding factor for project approvals.
• The ruling also reiterated the importance of public participation and expert committee assessments in environmental impact evaluations.
• This decision is expected to set a precedent for future large-scale developmental projects requiring environmental sanctions.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Judiciary
Environmental protection acts
Sustainable development
prelimsPoints: The Supreme Court's role in environmental jurisprudence.
Key provisions of the Environment Protection Act, 1986.
Concept of 'Public Trust Doctrine' in environmental law.
Types of environmental clearances required for large projects.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in balancing economic development and environmental conservation in India.
Analyze the role of the judiciary in strengthening environmental governance and ensuring sustainable development.
Evaluate the effectiveness of Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) processes in India and suggest reforms.
Examine the implications of such judicial pronouncements on future infrastructure projects and investment.
GS2.imageDescription: Illustration of Supreme Court building with a backdrop of a green forest and infrastructure project, symbolizing balance.
GS2.trivia: India was one of the first countries to make environmental protection a constitutional mandate through the 42nd Amendment Act, 1976.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Strategic Partnership Summit Concludes: Focus on Trade, Maritime Security, and Digital Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded with leaders reaffirming their commitment to strengthening the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership.
• Key discussions revolved around boosting bilateral trade to achieve the USD 200 billion target, enhancing maritime security cooperation in the Indo-Pacific, and promoting digital transformation.
• India emphasized its 'Act East' policy, reiterating support for ASEAN centrality in the regional architecture.
• Agreements were signed on cooperation in renewable energy, smart cities, and public health.
• The summit underscored the importance of a free, open, and rules-based Indo-Pacific region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: ASEAN founding members and current members.
Pillars of India-ASEAN cooperation.
Significance of the Indo-Pacific region.
Key initiatives under India's Act East Policy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of India’s 'Act East' policy and its significance for regional stability and economic growth.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in India-ASEAN economic cooperation, particularly in the post-pandemic era.
Examine India's role in promoting maritime security and a rules-based order in the Indo-Pacific region.
Evaluate the strategic importance of ASEAN for India's foreign policy and security interests.
GS2.imageDescription: Leaders of India and ASEAN member states shaking hands at a summit.
GS2.trivia: ASEAN was established on 8 August 1967 in Bangkok, Thailand, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration).

GS2.headline: Government Launches 'Gram Samriddhi' Scheme to Boost Rural Livelihoods and Infrastructure
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Government launched the 'Gram Samriddhi' scheme, a comprehensive initiative aimed at enhancing rural livelihoods and developing critical infrastructure in villages.
• The scheme focuses on improving access to credit for self-help groups (SHGs), promoting skill development, and supporting micro-enterprises.
• It integrates various existing rural development programs to ensure synergistic implementation and maximize impact.
• Special provisions are included for vulnerable sections and tribal areas to ensure inclusive growth.
• The scheme targets creation of sustainable employment opportunities and reduction of rural-urban migration.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
keyTerms: Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM)
prelimsPoints: Name of the scheme: 'Gram Samriddhi'.
Primary objective: enhancing rural livelihoods and infrastructure.
Target beneficiaries: rural population, SHGs, micro-enterprises.
Key focus areas: credit access, skill development, integrated programs.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of rural development schemes in achieving inclusive growth and poverty alleviation in India.
Analyze the potential of the 'Gram Samriddhi' scheme to address challenges like unemployment and migration in rural areas.
Evaluate the role of Self-Help Groups (SHGs) in empowering rural women and fostering economic development.
Suggest measures to improve the implementation and effectiveness of integrated rural development programs in India.
GS2.imageDescription: A vibrant village scene depicting people engaged in various livelihood activities and modern infrastructure.
GS2.trivia: Mahatma Gandhi's vision of 'Gram Swaraj' emphasized self-sufficient and self-governing villages as the foundation of India's development.

GS2.headline: UN Report Highlights India's Progress in SDG 4 (Quality Education) but Flags Challenges
GS2.explainer:
• A recent UN report acknowledged India's significant strides towards achieving Sustainable Development Goal 4 (Quality Education), particularly in increasing access to primary education.
• The report praised initiatives like the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 and Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan for improving enrollment rates and infrastructure.
• However, it also pointed out persistent challenges, including disparities in learning outcomes, digital divide, and quality of higher education.
• The report recommended increased investment in teacher training, early childhood care, and technology integration to bridge existing gaps.
• India's efforts are crucial for global progress towards SDG 4, given its large youth population.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: SDG 4: Quality Education.
Key Indian policies mentioned: NEP 2020, Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan.
Challenges highlighted: learning outcomes, digital divide, higher education quality.
UN as the source of the report.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's progress and challenges in achieving Sustainable Development Goal 4 (Quality Education).
Discuss how the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 aims to address the identified gaps in India's education system.
Examine the impact of the digital divide on educational equity in India, especially in the context of online learning.
Suggest policy interventions to improve learning outcomes and ensure inclusive and equitable quality education for all.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a classroom, with the UN logo subtly in the background, symbolizing global goals.
GS2.trivia: The 17 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) were adopted by all UN member states in 2015 as part of the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education

GS2.headline: New Draft Data Protection Rules Released for Public Consultation
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has released the draft rules under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023, for public feedback.
• These rules elaborate on the procedures for data fiduciaries to process personal data, mechanism for grievance redressal, and obligations of data processors.
• Key provisions include detailed guidelines on data breach notification, consent requirements, and the establishment of the Data Protection Board of India.
• The move aims to operationalize the DPDP Act, ensuring robust data privacy and security for Indian citizens.
• Stakeholders have been invited to submit their comments and suggestions within a 30-day period.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Cyber security
Role of IT in governance
GS2.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
prelimsPoints: Act under which rules are framed: Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
Ministry responsible: MeitY.
Key entity to be established: Data Protection Board of India.
Concept of 'data fiduciary' and 'data principal'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, in safeguarding citizen privacy in the digital age.
Analyze the key provisions of the newly released draft rules and their potential impact on businesses and individuals.
Examine the challenges in implementing a robust data protection framework in a digitally transforming economy like India's.
Evaluate the balance struck between individual privacy rights and legitimate state/business interests under the new law.
GS2.imageDescription: Digital lock icon with binary code, symbolizing data security and privacy.
GS2.trivia: The right to privacy was declared a fundamental right by the Supreme Court of India in the Puttaswamy judgment of 2017.

GS2.headline: Centre-State Financial Relations: 16th Finance Commission Holds Consultations with States
GS2.explainer:
• The 16th Finance Commission, currently underway, held its latest round of consultations with various state governments to understand their fiscal positions and recommendations.
• Discussions focused on the devolution of taxes, grants-in-aid, and measures to improve the fiscal health of states and local bodies.
• States presented their revenue projections, expenditure needs, and specific requests for sector-specific grants and disaster relief funds.
• The Commission's recommendations are crucial for shaping India's federal fiscal architecture for the next five years.
• Key areas of deliberation include horizontal and vertical devolution formulae, performance-based incentives, and debt management.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Finance Commission – its functions.
prelimsPoints: Constitutional article for Finance Commission: Article 280.
Current Finance Commission: 16th.
Key functions: recommending devolution of taxes, grants-in-aid.
Period of recommendations: five years.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and significance of the Finance Commission in shaping fiscal federalism in India.
Analyze the key challenges faced by states in managing their finances and their expectations from the 16th Finance Commission.
Examine the evolving dynamics of Centre-State financial relations, especially in the context of GST and increased devolution.
Suggest reforms to enhance the effectiveness of the Finance Commission's recommendations and improve fiscal discipline across all tiers of government.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian currency notes arranged to represent Centre-State financial flow, with the Finance Commission logo.
GS2.trivia: The first Finance Commission was constituted in 1951 under the chairmanship of K.C. Neogy.

GS2.headline: Election Commission of India Proposes Measures to Enhance Transparency in Political Funding
GS2.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) has submitted a set of proposals to the government aimed at bringing greater transparency to political funding.
• The recommendations include capping anonymous donations, mandating disclosure of donors for all contributions, and strengthening the audit of political parties' accounts.
• ECI highlighted concerns regarding the opacity of electoral bonds and the need for comprehensive reforms to ensure a level playing field.
• The proposals are part of ECI's ongoing efforts to streamline electoral processes and reduce the influence of 'black money' in elections.
• Implementation of these reforms would require amendments to existing laws, including the Representation of the People Act, 1951.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
prelimsPoints: Body proposing reforms: ECI.
Key area of reforms: political funding.
Specific proposals: capping anonymous donations, donor disclosure.
Act needing amendment: Representation of the People Act, 1951.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges posed by opaque political funding to democratic governance in India.
Analyze the Election Commission of India's role in promoting free and fair elections and its efforts towards electoral reforms.
Evaluate the effectiveness of electoral bonds in enhancing transparency in political funding and suggest alternatives.
Examine the constitutional and legal framework governing political funding in India and propose comprehensive reforms.
GS2.imageDescription: A ballot box with currency notes, symbolizing the intersection of elections and money, with ECI logo.
GS2.trivia: Article 324 of the Indian Constitution provides for an Election Commission to superintend, direct and control elections.

GS2.headline: Nationwide Mental Health Awareness Campaign Launched Across States
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, in collaboration with state governments, launched a comprehensive nationwide mental health awareness campaign.
• The campaign aims to destigmatize mental illness, promote early intervention, and improve access to mental healthcare services.
• It involves public workshops, digital campaigns, and training for frontline health workers to identify and support individuals with mental health concerns.
• Emphasis is placed on adolescent mental health and addressing the psychological impact of modern lifestyles.
• The initiative aligns with the National Mental Health Program and aims to bridge the treatment gap in India.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population
GS2.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: Ministry involved: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare.
Objective: destigmatize mental illness, promote early intervention.
Key focus groups: adolescents, frontline health workers.
Related program: National Mental Health Program.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing burden of mental health issues in India and the challenges in providing adequate mental healthcare services.
Analyze the importance of public awareness campaigns in destigmatizing mental illness and promoting help-seeking behavior.
Examine the provisions of the Mental Healthcare Act, 2017, and its impact on protecting the rights of individuals with mental illness.
Suggest comprehensive strategies to strengthen India's mental health infrastructure and integrate mental healthcare into primary health services.
GS2.imageDescription: Diverse group of people in a circle, symbolizing community support for mental well-being, with a green ribbon.
GS2.trivia: The World Health Organization (WHO) defines mental health as 'a state of well-being in which an individual realizes his or her own abilities, can cope with the normal stresses of life, can work productively and fruitfully, and is able to make a contribution to his or her community.'

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Justice
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health

GS2.headline: India and Brazil Strengthen South-South Cooperation with New Agreements
GS2.explainer:
• India and Brazil held high-level bilateral talks, reaffirming their commitment to strengthening South-South Cooperation and multilateralism.
• New agreements were signed focusing on cooperation in agriculture technology, renewable energy, and space exploration.
• Both nations emphasized the importance of reforming global institutions like the UN Security Council and the WTO to reflect current global realities.
• Discussions also covered enhancing trade ties, investment flows, and collaboration on climate change initiatives.
• This partnership is crucial for amplifying the voice of the Global South on international platforms.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: Countries involved: India and Brazil.
Key concept: South-South Cooperation.
Areas of cooperation: agriculture, renewable energy, space.
Global institutions mentioned: UN Security Council, WTO.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of South-South Cooperation in shaping the new world order and India's role in it.
Analyze the potential for India-Brazil partnership to drive economic growth and technological innovation in the Global South.
Examine the common positions of India and Brazil on reforming global governance institutions and their implications for multilateralism.
Evaluate the strategic importance of BRICS and other groupings in enhancing the influence of emerging economies.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Brazil intertwined, symbolizing strong bilateral relations.
GS2.trivia: India, Brazil, and South Africa (IBSA) form a unique trilateral cooperation forum to promote South-South Cooperation.

GS3.headline: Tamil Nadu Train Collision: 19 Injured, Rail Safety Concerns Resurface
GS3.explainer:
• A Mysuru-Darbhanga train collided with a freight train on a loop line near Chennai, resulting in injuries to 19 passengers.
• The incident occurred due to a suspected signalling error or human oversight, prompting a high-level inquiry by railway authorities.
• This collision has reignited debates over the need for advanced safety mechanisms like Kavach (Train Collision Avoidance System) across the entire rail network.
• Railway Minister assured swift action and compensation for the injured, emphasizing zero tolerance for safety lapses.
• The accident highlights the persistent challenges in maintaining operational safety on India's vast and busy railway network.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Railways
Disaster and disaster management
Internal security challenges
prelimsPoints: Location of the collision: Chennai, Tamil Nadu.
Number of injured: 19.
Name of the train involved: Mysuru-Darbhanga Express.
Kavach system: indigenous automatic train protection system developed by RDSO.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major challenges faced by Indian Railways in ensuring passenger safety and suggest measures to overcome them.
Analyze the role of technology, such as Kavach, in enhancing railway safety and its implementation challenges.
Examine the institutional mechanisms for investigating railway accidents and implementing recommendations.
Evaluate the human factor in railway accidents and the importance of training and compliance with safety protocols.
GS3.imageDescription: Emergency services attending to a derailed train coach at a collision site.
GS3.trivia: Indian Railways is the fourth-largest railway network in the world, transporting billions of passengers annually.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Economic and Social Development
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Energy, Ports, Roads, Railways, Airports, etc.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Inflation Concerns, Signals Cautious Stance
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach to manage persistent inflationary pressures, particularly in food prices, while supporting economic growth.
• RBI Governor highlighted global uncertainties and domestic demand-supply dynamics as key factors influencing the policy stance.
• The central bank reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the 4% target within a flexible inflation targeting framework.
• Experts anticipate continued vigilance from RBI, with future rate cuts dependent on a sustained decline in inflation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: Current Repo Rate: 6.5%.
Composition of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC).
Primary objective of RBI's monetary policy.
Definition of Repo Rate and its impact on lending rates.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in managing inflation while supporting economic growth in India.
Analyze the effectiveness of the flexible inflation targeting framework in achieving price stability in India.
Examine the various tools of monetary policy used by the RBI and their impact on the Indian economy.
Evaluate the role of global economic factors and domestic supply-side issues in influencing India's inflation trajectory.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI building with financial graphs overlaid, depicting economic indicators.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in 2016 and consists of six members: three officials of the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful autonomous landing experiment of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) 'Pushpak' at the Aeronautical Test Range in Chitradurga.
• This test marks a significant step towards developing a cost-effective and sustainable space transportation system for India.
• The RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) program aims to master critical technologies like hypersonic flight, autonomous landing, and winged body re-entry.
• Successful development of RLV technology will enable ISRO to reduce launch costs significantly and enhance India's competitive edge in the global space market.
• Future missions will involve orbital re-entry and return flight experiments.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Name of ISRO's RLV-TD: Pushpak.
Location of the test: Aeronautical Test Range, Chitradurga.
Key technologies demonstrated: autonomous landing, re-entry.
Objective of RLV program: cost reduction, sustainability.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology for India's space program and its potential economic benefits.
Analyze how ISRO's indigenous development of space technology contributes to India's strategic autonomy and global standing.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for India in the commercial space sector, particularly with technologies like RLV.
Evaluate the role of space technology in national development and security.
GS3.imageDescription: ISRO's 'Pushpak' Reusable Launch Vehicle gliding for an autonomous landing.
GS3.trivia: ISRO's first RLV-TD test was conducted in 2016, marking a significant milestone in India's space journey.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Science and Technology
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Space missions, satellites and their applications

GS3.headline: Defence Ministry Approves Indigenous Procurement of Naval Utility Helicopters
GS3.explainer:
• The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC), chaired by the Defence Minister, approved the procurement of a batch of indigenously designed and manufactured Naval Utility Helicopters (NUH).
• This decision is a major boost to the 'Make in India' and 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives in the defence sector.
• The NUHs will replace the aging fleet of Chetak helicopters, enhancing the operational capabilities of the Indian Navy for reconnaissance, search & rescue, and light transport missions.
• The procurement will involve significant participation from Indian private sector defence manufacturers, fostering domestic industry growth.
• This move aligns with the long-term vision of achieving self-reliance in critical defence technologies and reducing import dependency.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Various security forces and agencies and their mandate
Indigenization of technology
prelimsPoints: Body approving procurement: Defence Acquisition Council (DAC).
Type of helicopter: Naval Utility Helicopter (NUH).
Policy initiatives promoted: Make in India, Aatmanirbhar Bharat.
Helicopters being replaced: Chetak helicopters.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenization in India's defence sector for national security and economic growth.
Analyze the progress and challenges of the 'Make in India' and 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' initiatives in achieving self-reliance in defence production.
Examine the role of private sector participation in boosting India's defence manufacturing capabilities.
Evaluate the strategic implications of modernizing the Indian Navy's fleet with advanced indigenous platforms.
GS3.imageDescription: A modern naval utility helicopter flying over a warship at sea.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the world's largest importers of defence equipment, but efforts are underway to significantly increase domestic production.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Various security forces and agencies and their mandate
